summary: Recent updates to the negotiation engine in Firefox have improved its handling of Real-time Transport Protocol (RTP) extensions, specifically regarding the use of the RTX (retransmission) and RED (RTP payload format for redundancy) formats. These changes ensure that **the absence of a specified RTX payload type no longer triggers an error**, providing a smoother experience in scenarios such as video conferencing where network conditions can be variable. This enhancement allows for more flexibility in how Firefox handles media streams, ultimately improving the performance and reliability of **WebRTC, the technology behind video calls**.
